summary: Hardened the madobectl usage surface by listing the already-dispatched video-smoke send and receive entry points in top-level usage, with focused usage-error tests that avoid sender and receiver execution.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Top-level madobectl usage includes the video-smoke command entry point without running sender or receiver logic.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: apps/cli/src/lib.rs usage now lists `madobectl video-smoke send` and `madobectl video-smoke receive`; the new top-level usage test exercises a usage-error path only.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Parser and usage tests cover unknown top-level commands and the video-smoke usage surface.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Existing unknown top-level command coverage remains in apps/cli/src/lib.rs, and new tests cover top-level video-smoke usage plus video-smoke usage errors in crates/transport/src/video_smoke/cli.rs.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Existing display and hello behavior remains unchanged.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `nix develop -c cargo test -p madobectl` passed with existing hello and display tests.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Rust and repo hygiene checks pass.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `nix develop -c cargo test -p madobectl`, `nix develop -c cargo test -p madobe-transport video_smoke`, `nix develop -c just check`, and `git diff --check` passed in evidence/m4-madobectl-usage-surface-hardening/commands.log.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product runtime, capture, encode, transport, decode, render, presentation, cross-device, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Only CLI usage text, usage tests, and node evidence were changed; evidence/m4-madobectl-usage-surface-hardening/notes.md records the boundary.

commandsRun.command: nix develop -c cargo test -p madobe-transport video_smoke

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only changes CLI usage text and usage-error tests.
